sections.title: What is Posture?
points: Posture is the way a person positions their body while sitting, standing, or walking.
Good Posture: Keeps the spine in its natural S-shape and allows internal organs to work without being crushed.
Importance: Prevents bone deformation, backaches, and ensures efficient Respiration and Circulation.

sections.title: Common Postural Disorders
points: Scoliosis: An abnormal sideways curvature of the spine (often caused by carrying heavy bags on one shoulder).
Kyphosis: An excessive outward curve of the upper back, leading to a hunchback appearance.
Lordosis: An excessive inward curve of the lower back (swayback).

sections.title: Anatomy of a Tooth
points: Enamel: The white, outer part. It is the hardest substance in the human body.
Dentine: The bone-like layer under the enamel that protects the pulp.
Pulp Cavity: The living center containing blood vessels (for food/oxygen) and nerves (for pain).
Cementum: The tissue that anchors the tooth root to the jawbone.

sections.title: Dental Health Habits
points: Minerals: Teeth need Calcium and Phosphorus to stay hard.
Fluoride: Use fluoridated toothpaste to strengthen enamel against Dental Caries (cavities).
Diet: Avoid sugary foods which bacteria turn into acid to dissolve teeth.

sections.text: Exam Tip: Calcium and Vitamin D are the 'Best Friends' of the skeleton. Calcium builds the house, and Vitamin D is the key that lets it into the body!

cards.fact: The hardest part of the human body.

cards.mnemonic: Scoliosis makes the spine look like an 'S'!

cards.fact: The part of the tooth that feels pain.
